frameworks.name: vascular space occupancy (VASO) fMRI
frameworks.one_line: inversion-recovery sequence yielding vein-signal-free cerebral blood volume contrast

frameworks.name: layer-fMRI / sub-millimeter cortical depth imaging
frameworks.one_line: sub-millimeter functional imaging resolving cortical layer-specific activity

frameworks.name: cerebral blood volume (CBV) contrast
frameworks.one_line: CBV-sensitive contrast as alternative to BOLD draining-vein bias

core_question: Can vein-signal-free VASO layer-fMRI be feasibly implemented and characterized at widely accessible 3T scanners?
key_assumptions: longer T2* and stronger relative T1 contrast at 3T can offset reduced z-magnetization in inversion-recovery VASO
vein-free CBV contrast is preferable to BOLD for layer-specific signal
conventional scan durations are acceptable for layer-fMRI feasibility
manipulations: task domain (motor, visual stimulation, whole-brain movie-watching, cognitive tasks)
sequence implementation parameters for VASO at 3T
key_findings: longer T2* and stronger T1 contrast at 3T partly compensate for lower z-magnetization in IR-VASO
3T layer-fMRI VASO is feasible within conventional scan durations for motor tasks despite thermal noise limits
protocol generalizes across visual, movie-watching, and weaker-effect cognitive paradigms
interpretations: vein-free CBV layer-fMRI can be brought to widely accessible 3T scanners beyond privileged UHF centers
thermal noise is the primary limiting factor for 3T layer-VASO applications
limitations_noted: weaker effect sizes in cognitive tasks remain challenging
